D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Phycoerythrin — Red Algae's Green-Light Strategy

deep red seaweed absorbs green-yellow light (~565 nm) that penetrates further than blue — chlorophyll alone can't, so phycoerythrin antennas extend the depth range

Seawater filters red light first (gone by 10 m) and blue penetrates further; green sits in the middle, still abundant at 30-50 m. Chlorophyll absorbs poorly there. Red algae carry phycoerythrin — a blue protein-bound bilin pigment with absorption peak ~565 nm — that catches green light and Förster-transfers to chlorophyll. The pigment's red colour (complement of green) is what gives "red" algae their name.

Drag depth from surface to 50 m. The incident light spectrum red-shifts to blue-green; chlorophyll-only absorption stays poor; phycoerythrin captures the middle band.
